title: 4 Processing a Provincial Concentrator Deposit
body_text:
4	Processing a Provincial Concentrator Deposit
4.1	Overview of Processing a Provincial Concentrator Account Deposit
Your financial institution processes deposits made by the Province of BC, a BC Services office, or businesses on behalf of the Province of BC, to the appropriate Provincial Concentrator holding account held at your financial institution.
Report the total value of cash and/or cheques received to Central 1 using the Province of BC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow. You must submit the Province of BC Deposit Slip the same day you receive a deposit.
Central 1 uses the information to sweep the Provincial Concentrator holding accounts held at your financial institution and aggregates the deposits to credit the Province of BC. Specifically, Central 1 issues a pre-authorized debit (PAD) against each Provincial Concentrator holding account and credits the funds to the Province of BCâs account at Central 1.
4.2	Verifying and Processing Provincial Concentrator Account Deposits
Verify and process a Provincial Concentrator holding account deposit as follows:
Ensure that all cheques are acceptable for deposit (e.g., verify the date, itemâs MICR, signature). Sort items by currency if applicable. See the Deposit Processing Part for more information.
Ensure that all cheques are endorsed with the Provincial Concentrator holding account endorsement stamp. The stamp provides the Location ID and return routing information (CIBC transit 00090-010) if items are dishonoured. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Endorsement Stamp
Convert the total of any US cash or cheques to Canadian dollars using the daily posted exchange rate. Retain a copy for your records. Provincial Concentrator holding accounts are in Canadian dollars.
Credit the total value of Canadian cheques and/or cash to the appropriate Provincial Concentrator holding account. Follow your internal policies and procedures. If multiple deposits are received from an agent, post the deposit slip totals to the account separately.
Credit the total Canadian dollar equivalent of any US dollar cheques and/or cash into the appropriate Provincial Concentrator holding account. Follow your internal policies and procedures. If multiple deposits are received from an agent, post the deposit slip totals to the account separately.
Prepare and process the cheques as a separate deposit in Branch Capture. If you have US cheques to deposit, create a separate US deposit in Branch Capture. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on creating deposits in Branch Capture. Ensure that you:
â
Record âProvincial Concentrator Accountâ in one of the  Deposit Reference fields when creating the deposit in Branch Capture.
â
Enter the CIBC transit number (00090-010) in the Deposit Transit and Deposit Route fields for each Provincial Concentrator Account deposit item in Branch Capture. This is important because dishonoured Provincial Concentrator Account deposit items must be returned to CIBC for processing. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on entering return information.
Note:	Failure to enter the correct return transit will result in the items being returned to your financial institution. See Section 5.3, Managing Chargebacks for Provincial Concentrator Holding Account, for more information.
Submit a Province of BC Deposit Slip in Service Now. See Section 4.3, Submitting a Province of BC Deposit Slip Request in ServiceNow, for procedures.
4.3 	Submitting a Province of BC Deposit Slip Request in ServiceNow
Use ServiceNow to submit a Province of BC Deposit Slip on the day you receive the deposit. Failure to do so will prevent Central 1 from issuing a PAD to debit the Province of BC holding account and from crediting the Province of BCâs account held at Central 1.
Note:	If you donât have access to ServiceNow, contact your User Management Security Officer to request access.
Submit a Province of BC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 2.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 2: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
, then select
ServiceNow
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select âService Catalogueâ
Select
Province of BC Deposit Slip
in the
Paper Payments
category. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select âProvince of BC Deposit Slipâ
Complete the Province of BC Deposit Slip page as follows. See Figure 6. Your financial institution representativeâs name pre-populates.
Important
:	If you are processing more than one deposit from the agent, you must enter each deposit separately. You can enter multiple deposits in one Province of BC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow by adding additional rows as described.
Note: 	Mandatory fields have an asterisk (*).
â
Select the appropriate Location ID from the drop-down menu.
â¡
Enter the deposit amount. The amount should represent the total of all cash and/or cheques received in Canadian dollars. The amount that you enter is used in the account sweep that debits the Provincial Concentrator holding account and credits the Province of BC.
â¢
Select
Credit
from the Type drop-down menu.
â£
Enter a note (optional).
â¤
Select the plus sign (+) to add a new row and include an additional deposit.
Note: 	Select the (
â¨¯
) sign o delete a row.
â¥
Select the paper clip icon to attach items (if applicable).
â¦
Select
Submit
. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.  Alternatively, select the trash can to delete all data and start over.
Figure 6: Province of BC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow
END
